# Shell Over Edge

Temporary shell access over HTTPS.

## Flow

- Ask the user to start an agent on the target.
- POSIX: `curl -sS https://soe.stoff.dev | sh`
- PowerShell: `irm https://soe.stoff.dev | iex`
- Use the printed 8-character code.
- Send raw: `curl -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/<code>/send --data 'pwd'`
- Send with cwd: `curl -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/<code>/send --data '{"body":"pwd","cwd":"/tmp"}'`
- Add `?timeout=10` only for a custom timeout.
- Read successful `/send` responses as plain text.
- End: `curl -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/<code>/end`

## API

- `GET /`: POSIX agent by default, PowerShell when `User-Agent` or `Accept` contains `PowerShell` or `pwsh`.
- `POST /<code>/send`: send one command; body is raw text or `{"body":"pwd","cwd":"/tmp"}`.
- `POST /<code>/send?timeout=10`: send one command with a custom timeout.
- `POST /<code>/end`: close the session.

## Rules

- No bearer tokens, URL tokens, or invented codes.
- Default timeout is 30 seconds. Custom timeout belongs in `?timeout=10`; JSON only supports `body` and `cwd`.
- Do not call retired endpoints: `/commands`, `/events`, `/upload`, `/download`, `/api/agent/*`, `/start/*`, `/connect.sh`.
- Keep commands explicit and reversible. Close sessions with `/end`.

## Limits

TTL 2 hours. Command body 64 KB. Result body 1 MB. Timeout 1-50 seconds.
